source_topic: How a transformer 'pays attention' — the architecture behind ChatGPT

scenes.narration: In 2017, eight researchers wrote a twelve-page paper that became the engine behind every major AI you use. Their idea: attention.
Take the word 'bank.' River or vault? The model doesn't guess — it scores every surrounding word simultaneously. 'Money' pulls the highest weight. Ambiguity dissolved.

props.fallbackText: Each word scores every other word — the highest-weight words define the meaning

scenes.narration: Before this, models read word by word — early context faded. Attention eliminates that: every word queries every other word simultaneously.
Each word fires a Query. Others answer with Keys — bids for relevance. Top matches return their Values, blended into one context-rich output vector.
This runs across many parallel heads simultaneously. One tracks grammar, another meaning, another position. All see the full sentence but learn completely different patterns.
GPT-3 runs ninety-six heads simultaneously, per token, across twelve thousand dimensions. That's what paying attention looks like at scale.

props.label: running in parallel per token — 12,288 dimensions each

scenes.narration: One of the paper's eight authors just joined OpenAI to design what comes after this. Follow @yourhandle for one concept every day.

caption:
Eight researchers wrote a paper that became the brain of every AI you use. This is the idea inside it.

Every word in your prompt simultaneously asks every other word: "do you matter to understanding me?"

'Bank' next to 'money'? Financial. 'Bank' next to 'river'? Geography. One equation resolves it instantly.

🧠 One of those eight researchers just joined OpenAI to build what comes after attention.

Save this for the next time someone asks you how ChatGPT actually works.
